title: pelargonium × ludovicianum
intro: Pelargonium × ludovicianum, also known as the Rose Geranium or Pelargonium Roseum, is a species of flowering plant in the geranium family, native to South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to balance hormones, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. The Rose Geranium has been traditionally used to treat menstrual cramps, anxiety, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote emotional balance, soothe skin irritations, and support overall well-being.
